mnemonics: ALLigators Exist in the Domain (∀ for All, ∃ for Exists)
Flip the Quantifier, Negate the Predicate (for negating statements)
Some Are, All Are Not (to remember ∃¬ is not the same as ¬∀)
keyPoints: The universal quantifier (∀) means 'for all' or 'for every' element in a domain.
The existential quantifier (∃) means 'there exists' at least one element in a domain.
The domain of discourse is crucial, as it defines the set of objects over which the quantifiers range.
commonMistakes: Misinterpreting the scope of a quantifier, especially when multiple quantifiers are used (order matters).
Incorrectly negating quantified statements (e.g., thinking ¬∀x P(x) is the same as ∀x ¬P(x), when it is actually ∃x ¬P(x)).
